pragma: no cover\n    EventBus = _DummyBus  # type: ignore\n\n\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.theme import ThemeManager\nexcept Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n    ThemeManager = _DummyTheme  # type: ignore\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Pixmap caching helpers\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass _PixmapCache:\n    \"\"\"\n    Very small LRU cache that stores pixmaps keyed by absolute path.\n    Prevents re-decoding identical images when the avatar is shown in multiple\n    locations at once.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _MAX_ENTRIES = 32\n    _store: OrderedDict[str, QPixmap] = OrderedDict()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def get(cls, path: str) -> Optional[QPixmap]:\n        try:\n            pixmap = cls._store.pop(path)\n            cls._store[path] = pixmap  # move to end (most recently used)\n            return pixmap\n        except KeyError:\n            return None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def put(cls, path: str, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n        cls._store[path] = pixmap\n        cls._store.move_to_end(path)\n        # evict least recently used\n        while len(cls._store) > cls._MAX_ENTRIES:\n            cls._store.popitem(last=False)\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Asynchronous loader used to decode / scale the avatar off the UI thread\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass _AvatarLoader(QRunnable):\n    \"\"\"\n    Decode and scale a QPixmap in a thread-pool to avoid UI jank when very\n    large images are supplied.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        path: str,\n        target_size: QSize,\n        on_loaded: \"Signal[QPixmap]\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self._path = path\n        self._target_size = target_size\n        self._on_loaded = on_loaded\n\n    def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        try:\n            image = QImage(self._path)\n            if image.isNull():\n                raise ValueError(f\"Unable to load image: {self._path}\")\n\n            # Preserve aspect ratio, crop to square, then scale\n            side = min(image.width(), image.height())\n            cropped = image.copy(\n                (image.width() - side) // 2,\n                (image.height() - side) // 2,\n                side,\n                side,\n            )\n            scaled = cropped.scaled(\n                self._target_size,\n                Qt.KeepAspectRatio,\n                Qt.SmoothTransformation,\n            )\n            pixmap = QPixmap.fromImage(scaled)\n            _PixmapCache.put(self._path, pixmap)\n            self._on_loaded.emit(pixmap)\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            log.exception(\"Failed to load avatar: %s\", self._path)\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Avatar widget\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass AvatarWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    Displays a round avatar with an optional presence badge.\n\n    Signals\n    -------\n    clicked: Signal[None]\n        Emitted on left mouse click.\n    avatarChanged: Signal[str]\n        Emitted when the user changes his / her avatar locally.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    clicked = Signal()\n    avatarChanged = Signal(str)\n\n    _DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST = {\n        \"image/png\",\n        \"image/jpeg\",\n        \"image/webp\",\n        \"image/gif\",\n    }\n    _DEFAULT_SIZE = 64\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        user_id: str,\n        *,\n        size: int | None = None,\n        event_bus: Optional[EventBus] = None,\n        theme_manager: Optional[ThemeManager] = None,\n        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(parent)\n\n        self.setAcceptDrops(True)\n        self.setCursor(Qt.PointingHandCursor)\n        self.setFixedSize(QSize(size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE, size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE))\n\n        # dependencies\n        self._user_id = user_id\n        self._event_bus = event_bus or _DummyBus()\n        self._theme = theme_manager or _DummyTheme()\n\n        # runtime state\n        self._avatar_path: Optional[str] = None\n        self._pixmap: Optional[QPixmap] = None\n        self._presence: str = \"offline\"  # offline, online, busy, away …\n        self._thread_pool = QThreadPool.globalInstance()\n\n        self._install_event_listeners()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public API\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def avatar(self) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return absolute path of current avatar (may be None).\"\"\"\n        return self._avatar_path\n\n    def presence(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the current presence key (online / busy / …).\"\"\"\n        return self._presence\n\n    @Slot(str)\n    def set_avatar(self, path: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update the avatar from the given image path.\"\"\"\n        path = os.path.abspath(path)\n        if not os.path.exists(path):\n            log.warning(\"Avatar path does not exist: %s\", path)\n            return\n\n        self._avatar_path = path\n        cached = _PixmapCache.get(path)\n        if cached is not None:\n            self._pixmap = cached\n            self.update()\n        else:\n            loader = _AvatarLoader(\n                path=path,\n                target_size=self.size(),\n                on_loaded=self._on_async_loaded,\n            )\n            self._thread_pool.start(loader)\n\n    @Slot(str)\n    def set_presence(self, state: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update presence overlay, e.g. from the event-bus.\"\"\"\n        if state == self._presence:\n            return\n        self._presence = state\n        self.update()\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Event handling\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def paintEvent(self, event):  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Paint circular avatar with presence badge.\"\"\"\n        painter = QPainter(self)\n        painter.setRenderHint(QPainter.Antialiasing)\n\n        if self._pixmap and not self._pixmap.isNull():\n            painter.setBrush(Qt.NoBrush)\n            path = self._rounded_rect_path()\n            painter.setClipPath(path)\n            painter.drawPixmap(self.rect(), self._pixmap)\n        else:\n            # fallback placeholder\n            painter.setBrush(\n                self._theme.color(\"avatar.background\", \"#8F8F8F\")\n            )\n            painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n            painter.drawEllipse(self.rect())\n\n        self._draw_presence_indicator(painter)\n\n    def mousePressEvent(self, event: QMouseEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        if event.button() == Qt.LeftButton:\n            self.clicked.emit()\n        super().mousePressEvent(event)\n\n    def dragEnterEvent(self, event: QDragEnterEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        if any(fmt in event.mimeData().formats() for fmt in self._DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST):\n            event.acceptProposedAction()\n\n    def dropEvent(self, event: QDropEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        url: QUrl\n        for url in event.mimeData().urls():\n            if url.isLocalFile():\n                self._handle_new_avatar(Path(url.toLocalFile()))\n                event.acceptProposedAction()\n                return\n        QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Invalid file\", \"Please drop a local image file.\")\n\n    def contextMenuEvent(self, event: QContextMenuEvent):  # noqa: D401\n        menu = QMenu(self)\n\n        choose_action = QAction(\"Change avatar …\", self)\n        choose_action.triggered.connect(self._choose_avatar)\n        menu.addAction(choose_action)\n\n        menu.addSeparator()\n\n        if self._avatar_path:\n            reveal = QAction(\"Reveal in Explorer\", self)\n            reveal.triggered.connect(self._reveal_in_file_manager)\n            menu.addAction(reveal)\n\n        menu.exec(event.globalPos())\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal helpers\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _install_event_listeners(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to application-wide events: theme change, presence updates\n        for our user, etc.\n        \"\"\"\n        # presence update\n        if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n            self._event_bus.subscribe(\n                \"presence.changed\",\n                handler=lambda e: (\n                    self.set_presence(e.payload[\"state\"])\n                    if e.payload.get(\"user_id\") == self._user_id\n                    else None\n                ),\n            )\n\n        # theme hot reload\n        if isinstance(self._theme, ThemeManager):\n            self._theme.themeChanged.connect(lambda: self.update())  # type: ignore\n\n    @Slot(QPixmap)\n    def _on_async_loaded(self, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n        self._pixmap = pixmap\n        self.update()\n\n    def _rounded_rect_path(self):\n        \"\"\"Returns a QPainterPath of a circle matching widget size.\"\"\"\n        from PySide6.QtGui import QPainterPath\n\n        path = QPainterPath()\n        path.addEllipse(self.rect())\n        return path\n\n    def _draw_presence_indicator(self, painter: QPainter) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Paint a small presence dot on bottom-right corner.\"\"\"\n        colors = {\n            \"online\": \"#4CAF50\",\n            \"busy\": \"#F44336\",\n            \"away\": \"#FFB300\",\n            \"offline\": \"#9E9E9E\",\n        }\n        radius = max(6, self.width() // 6)\n        center = QPoint(self.width() - radius, self.height() - radius)\n\n        painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n        painter.setBrush(colors.get(self._presence, \"#9E9E9E\"))\n        painter.drawEllipse(center, radius, radius)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Context-menu actions\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _choose_avatar(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Open file-dialog for user to pick a new avatar picture.\"\"\"\n        filename, _ = QFileDialog.getOpenFileName(\n            self,\n            \"Select Avatar Picture\",\n            \"\",\n            \"Images (*.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp *.gif)\",\n        )\n        if filename:\n            self._handle_new_avatar(Path(filename))\n\n    def _handle_new_avatar(self, path: Path) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Validate and persist the chosen avatar (drag-drop or file-dialog).\"\"\"\n        if not path.exists():\n            return\n        if not path.suffix.lower() in {\".png\", \".jpg\", \".jpeg\", \".webp\", \".gif\"}:\n            QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Unsupported file\", \"File is not a supported image type.\")\n            return\n\n        self.set_avatar(str(path))\n        self.avatarChanged.emit(str(path))\n        # Publish to event-bus so other processes may sync the change\n        if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n            self._event_bus.publish(  # type: ignore\n                \"avatar.changed\",\n                user_id=self._user_id,\n                path=str(path),\n            )\n\n    def _reveal_in_file_manager(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Platform-specific file manager reveal of the avatar file.\"\"\"\n        if not self._avatar_path:\n            return\n        try:\n            if sys.platform.startswith(\"darwin\"):\n                from subprocess import call\n\n                call([\"open\", \"--\", self._avatar_path])\n            elif os.name == \"nt\":\n                os.startfile(self._avatar_path)  #      2→"""
     3→tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py
     4→
     5→End-to-end integration tests covering the profile-synchronisation layer that keeps
     6→user preferences in-sync between micro-front-ends and the cloud profile service.
     7→"""
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import asyncio
    11→import contextlib
    12→import time
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    14→from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional
    15→
    16→import pytest
    17→
    18→
    19→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    20→#                             ––– Test Doubles –––                            #
    21→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    22→class NetworkError(ConnectionError):
    23→    """Raised when the remote profile gateway is unreachable."""
    24→
    25→
    26→class FakeEventBus:
    27→    """
    28→    *Extremely* lightweight pub/sub bus for integration testing.
    29→
    30→    It behaves like the in-process event bus the real application would use,
    31→    but is limited to fan-out broadcasts with no routing / filtering logic.
    32→    """
    33→
    34→    def __init__(self) -> None:
    35→        self._subscribers: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}
    36→
    37→    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any) -> None:
    38→        """
    39→        Dispatch an event to all current subscribers.  Publishing is synchronous
    40→        but the individual consumers receive the events asynchronously via the
    41→        returned queue from :py:meth:`listen`.
    42→        """
    43→        if topic not in self._subscribers:  # pragma: no cover
    44→            return  # No one cares.
    45→
    46→        for queue in self._subscribers[topic]:
    47→            # Use put_nowait so a slow consumer does *not* block the publisher.
    48→            queue.put_nowait(payload)
    49→
    50→    def listen(self, topic: str) -> "asyncio.Queue[Any]":
    51→        """
    52→        Subscribe to a topic and receive a dedicated queue that will carry all
    53→        future events for that topic.
    54→        """
    55→        q: "asyncio.Queue[Any]" = asyncio.Queue()
    56→        self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, []).append(q)
    57→        return q
    58→
    59→
    60→class FakeRemoteGateway:
    61→    """
    62→    In-memory substitute of the cloud-backed profile service.
    63→
    64→    It emulates three main responsibilities:
    65→      1. Keeping the canonical *remote* profile for each user.
    66→      2. Detecting network availability and raising :class:`NetworkError`
    67→         accordingly.
    68→      3. Broadcasting updates from *any* client to *all* clients subscribed for
    69→         that user.
    70→    """
    71→
    72→    def __init__(self) -> None:
    73→        self._remote_profiles: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
    74→        self._channels: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}
    75→        self.offline: bool = False
    76→
    77→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    78→    #                        ––– Public API (client) –––                    #
    79→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    80→    async def push_profile(self, user_id: str, profile: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    81→        """Persist the profile to the 'cloud' and broadcast the change."""
    82→        self._ensure_online()
    83→
    84→        # Use a *monotonic* timestamp as a version indicator (LWW conflict
    85→        # resolution).
    86→        merged = dict(profile)
    87→        merged["_last_modified"] = time.monotonic()
    88→        self._remote_profiles[user_id] = merged
    89→
    90→        # Fan-out to listeners (non-blocking).
    91→        for q in self._channels.get(user_id, []):
    92→            q.put_nowait(merged)
    93→
    94→    def subscribe(self, user_id: str) -> "asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]":
    95→        """
    96→        Subscribe to server-side changes for *user_id* and receive a queue that
    97→        is fed whenever **another** client pushes a change.
    98→        """
    99→        q: "asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]" = asyncio.Queue()
   100→        self._channels.setdefault(user_id, []).append(q)
   101→        return q
   102→
   103→    def snapshot(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   104→        """Return the server-side profile for assertions."""
   105→        return self._remote_profiles.get(user_id, {}).copy()
   106→
   107→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   108→    #                           ––– Internals –––                        #
   109→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
   110→    def _ensure_online(self) -> None:
   111→        if self.offline:
   112→            raise NetworkError("Remote gateway is offline")
   113→
   114→
   115→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   116→#                       ––– System-Under-Test: Sync Service –––               #
   117→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   118→@dataclass(slots=True)
   119→class ProfileSyncService:
   120→    """
   121→    Minimal, yet realistic, profile-synchronisation service.
   122→
   123→    A real implementation would be heavily event-driven, but for the purpose of
   124→    integration testing we drive the important mechanics manually.
   125→    """
   126→
   127→    user_id: str
   128→    event_bus: FakeEventBus
   129→    gateway: FakeRemoteGateway
   130→
   131→    local_profile: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
   132→
   133→    # Internal run-state
   134→    _unsynced_changes: bool = field(default=False, init=False)
   135→    _running: bool = field(default=False, init=False)
   136→    _remote_listener_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = field(default=None, init=False)
   137→
   138→    # ------------------------------- Life-Cycle ----------------------------- #
   139→    async def start(self) -> None:
   140→        """Kick-off background tasks (remote listener)."""
   141→        if self._running:  # pragma: no cover
   142→            return
   143→        self._running = True
   144→        self._remote_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(self._listen_remote())
   145→
   146→    async def stop(self) -> None:
   147→        """Gracefully shut down the background listener."""
   148→        if not self._running:  # pragma: no cover
   149→            return
   150→
   151→        self._running = False
   152→        if self._remote_listener_task:
   153→            self._remote_listener_task.cancel()
   154→            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):
   155→                await self._remote_listener_task
   156→
   157→    # ---------------------------- Public Interface ------------------------- #
   158→    def update_local(self, changes: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   159→        """
   160→        Called by the UI layer whenever the user modifies their preferences.
   161→        """
   162→        self.local_profile.update(changes)
   163→        self._unsynced_changes = True
   164→
   165→        # Immediately broadcast to local components.
   166→        self.event_bus.publish(
   167→            f"profile.{self.user_id}.updated", dict(self.local_profile)
   168→        )
   169→
   170→        # Fire-and-forget the sync attempt.
   171→        asyncio.create_task(self._attempt_sync())
   172→
   173→    async def on_network_restored(self) -> None:
   174→        """
   175→        The connectivity monitor can poke this method once the network becomes
   176→        available again, so we can flush pending writes.
   177→        """
   178→        await self._attempt_sync()
   179→
   180→    # ------------------------------- Internals ----------------------------- #
   181→    async def _attempt_sync(self) -> None:
   182→        """Try to push local changes to the remote gateway."""
   183→        if not self._unsynced_changes:
   184→            return
   185→
   186→        try:
   187→            await self.gateway.push_profile(self.user_id, dict(self.local_profile))
   188→            self._unsynced_changes = False
   189→        except NetworkError:
   190→            # Silently keep the dirty flag so we know to retry later.
   191→            pass
   192→
   193→    async def _listen_remote(self) -> None:
   194→        """
   195→        Background coroutine that receives server-side updates that originated
   196→        from *other* clients.
   197→        """
   198→        q = self.gateway.subscribe(self.user_id)
   199→
   200→        while self._running:
   201→            remote_profile = await q.get()
   202→
   203→            # We only care when the incoming update carries a newer timestamp.
   204→            local_ts = self.local_profile.get("_last_modified", -1)
   205→            remote_ts = remote_profile["_last_modified"]
   206→            if remote_ts <= local_ts:
   207→                continue  # Local copy is newer or identical.
   208→
   209→            self.local_profile = dict(remote_profile)
   210→            self.event_bus.publish(
   211→                f"profile.{self.user_id}.updated", dict(self.local_profile)
   212→            )
   213→
   214→
   215→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   216→#                                ––– Helpers –––                              #
   217→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   218→async def wait_for(
   219→    queue: "asyncio.Queue[Any]",
   220→    *,
   221→    predicate: Callable[[Any], bool] | None = None,
   222→    timeout: float = 2.0,
   223→) -> Any:
   224→    """
   225→    Utility that awaits until `predicate(item)` is *True* for an element read
   226→    from *queue*.  If *predicate* is :pydata:`None`, the call returns the first
   227→    element received.
   228→    """
   229→    deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout
   230→    predicate = predicate or (lambda _x: True)
   231→
   232→    while True:
   233→        remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()
   234→        if remaining <= 0:
   235→            raise TimeoutError("Timeout while waiting for queue event")
   236→
   237→        item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=remaining)
   238→        if predicate(item):
   239→            return item
   240→

   245→@pytest.fixture()
   246→def event_bus() -> FakeEventBus:
   247→    return FakeEventBus()

   255→@pytest.fixture()
   256→async def services(event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway):
   257→    """
   258→    Spin-up *two* sync services to simulate two independent micro-front-ends
   259→    running in different processes.
   260→    """
   261→    svc_a = ProfileSyncService("alice", event_bus, remote_gateway)
   262→    svc_b = ProfileSyncService("alice", event_bus, remote_gateway)
   263→
   264→    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.start(), svc_b.start())
   265→    yield svc_a, svc_b
   266→
   267→    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.stop(), svc_b.stop())

   270→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   271→async def test_local_update_propagates_to_remote_and_peers(
   272→    services, event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway
   273→):
   274→    """
   275→    When one service updates the local profile, the change should:
   276→
   277→      1. Appear on the remote gateway.
   278→      2. Be broadcast on the local event bus.
   279→      3. Update *other* micro-front-ends listening for that user.
   280→    """
   281→    svc_a, svc_b = services
   282→    bus_q = event_bus.listen("profile.alice.updated")
   283→
   284→    # Act – change a preference on service A.
   285→    svc_a.update_local({"theme": "dark"})
   286→
   287→    # 1) Wait for the bus broadcast and verify the payload.
   288→    msg = await wait_for(bus_q, predicate=lambda m: m.get("theme") == "dark")
   289→    assert msg["theme"] == "dark"
   290→
   291→    # 2) Eventually, remote profile should be in sync.
   292→    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Allow async push to complete.
   293→    assert remote_gateway.snapshot("alice")["theme"] == "dark"
   294→
   295→    # 3) The peer (service B) must have updated its local copy.
   296→    assert svc_b.local_profile["theme"] == "dark"
   297→
   298→
   299→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   300→async def test_last_write_wins_conflict_resolution(
   301→    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway
   302→):
   303→    """
   304→    When concurrent updates occur, the service implementing a *later* change
   305→    (higher `_last_modified`) must win the conflict.
   306→    """
   307→    svc_a, svc_b = services
   308→
   309→    # Simulate near-simultaneous changes.
   310→    svc_a.update_local({"language": "en"})
   311→    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)
   312→    svc_b.update_local({"language": "fr"})  # <- later write wins.
   313→
   314→    # Give the sync loop a moment to converge.
   315→    await asyncio.sleep(0.2)
   316→
   317→    snap = remote_gateway.snapshot("alice")
   318→    assert snap["language"] == "fr"
   319→    assert svc_a.local_profile["language"] == svc_b.local_profile["language"] == "fr"
   320→
   321→
   322→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   323→async def test_offline_changes_are_synced_when_network_recovers(
   324→    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway
   325→):
   326→    """
   327→    Offline edits must be queued locally and flushed to the server once
   328→    connectivity is restored.
   329→    """
   330→    svc_a, _ = services
   331→
   332→    # Simulate loss of connectivity *before* the update.
   333→    remote_gateway.offline = True
   334→    svc_a.update_local({"showAvatars": False})
   335→
   336→    # Wait briefly to ensure the failed attempt has happened.
   337→    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
   338→    assert remote_gateway.snapshot("alice") == {}  # Nothing on the server.
   339→
   340→    # Bring the network back and trigger a retry.
   341→    remote_gateway.offline = False
   342→    await svc_a.on_network_restored()
   343→
   344→    # The push should succeed shortly after.
   345→    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
   346→    assert remote_gateway.snapshot("alice")["showAvatars"] is False
   347→    assert svc_a._unsynced_changes is False
   348→```

Publishing is synchronous\n        but the individual consumers receive the events asynchronously via the\n        returned queue from :py:meth:`listen`.\n        \"\"\"\n        if topic not in self._subscribers:  # pragma: no cover\n            return  # No one cares.\n\n        for queue in self._subscribers[topic]:\n            # Use put_nowait so a slow consumer does *not* block the publisher.\n            queue.put_nowait(payload)\n\n    def listen(self, topic: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to a topic and receive a dedicated queue that will carry all\n        future events for that topic.\n        \"\"\"\n        q: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n        self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, []).append(q)\n        return q\n\n\nclass FakeRemoteGateway:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-memory substitute of the cloud-backed profile service.\n\n    It emulates three main responsibilities:\n      1. Keeping the canonical *remote* profile for each user.\n      2. Detecting network availability and raising :class:`NetworkError`\n         accordingly.\n      3. Broadcasting updates from *any* client to *all* clients subscribed for\n         that user.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._remote_profiles: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\n        self._channels: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\n        self.offline: bool = False\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #                        ––– Public API (client) –––                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def push_profile(self, user_id: str, profile: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the profile to the 'cloud' and broadcast the change.\"\"\"\n        self._ensure_online()\n\n        # Use a *monotonic* timestamp as a version indicator (LWW conflict\n        # resolution).\n        merged = dict(profile)\n        merged[\"_last_modified\"] = time.monotonic()\n        self._remote_profiles[user_id] = merged\n\n        # Fan-out to listeners (non-blocking).\n        for q in self._channels.get(user_id, []):\n            q.put_nowait(merged)\n\n    def subscribe(self, user_id: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to server-side changes for *user_id* and receive a queue that\n        is fed whenever **another** client pushes a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        q: \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n        self._channels.setdefault(user_id, []).append(q)\n        return q\n\n    def snapshot(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Return the server-side profile for assertions.\"\"\"\n        return self._remote_profiles.get(user_id, {}).copy()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    #                           ––– Internals –––                        #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    def _ensure_online(self) -> None:\n        if self.offline:\n            raise NetworkError(\"Remote gateway is offline\")\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                       ––– System-Under-Test: Sync Service –––               #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass ProfileSyncService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Minimal, yet realistic, profile-synchronisation service.\n\n    A real implementation would be heavily event-driven, but for the purpose of\n    integration testing we drive the important mechanics manually.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    user_id: str\n    event_bus: FakeEventBus\n    gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n\n    local_profile: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    # Internal run-state\n    _unsynced_changes: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n    _running: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n    _remote_listener_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = field(default=None, init=False)\n\n    # ------------------------------- Life-Cycle ----------------------------- #\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Kick-off background tasks (remote listener).\"\"\"\n        if self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n            return\n        self._running = True\n        self._remote_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(self._listen_remote())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Gracefully shut down the background listener.\"\"\"\n        if not self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n            return\n\n        self._running = False\n        if self._remote_listener_task:\n            self._remote_listener_task.cancel()\n            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\n                await self._remote_listener_task\n\n    # ---------------------------- Public Interface ------------------------- #\n    def update_local(self, changes: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Called by the UI layer whenever the user modifies their preferences.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.local_profile.update(changes)\n        self._unsynced_changes = True\n\n        # Immediately broadcast to local components.\n        self.event_bus.publish(\n            f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n        )\n\n        # Fire-and-forget the sync attempt.\n        asyncio.create_task(self._attempt_sync())\n\n    async def on_network_restored(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        The connectivity monitor can poke this method once the network becomes\n        available again, so we can flush pending writes.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self._attempt_sync()\n\n    # ------------------------------- Internals ----------------------------- #\n    async def _attempt_sync(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Try to push local changes to the remote gateway.\"\"\"\n        if not self._unsynced_changes:\n            return\n\n        try:\n            await self.gateway.push_profile(self.user_id, dict(self.local_profile))\n            self._unsynced_changes = False\n        except NetworkError:\n            # Silently keep the dirty flag so we know to retry later.\n            pass\n\n    async def _listen_remote(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Background coroutine that receives server-side updates that originated\n        from *other* clients.\n        \"\"\"\n        q = self.gateway.subscribe(self.user_id)\n\n        while self._running:\n            remote_profile = await q.get()\n\n            # We only care when the incoming update carries a newer timestamp.\n            local_ts = self.local_profile.get(\"_last_modified\", -1)\n            remote_ts = remote_profile[\"_last_modified\"]\n            if remote_ts <= local_ts:\n                continue  # Local copy is newer or identical.\n\n            self.local_profile = dict(remote_profile)\n            self.event_bus.publish(\n                f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n            )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                                ––– Helpers –––                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nasync def wait_for(\n    queue: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\",\n    *,\n    predicate: Callable[[Any], bool] | None = None,\n    timeout: float = 2.0,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Utility that awaits until `predicate(item)` is *True* for an element read\n    from *queue*.  If *predicate* is :pydata:`None`, the call returns the first\n    element received.\n    \"\"\"\n    deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout\n    predicate = predicate or (lambda _x: True)\n\n    while True:\n        remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()\n        if remaining <= 0:\n            raise TimeoutError(\"Timeout while waiting for queue event\")\n\n        item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=remaining)\n        if predicate(item):\n            return item\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                            ––– Integration Tests –––                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef event_bus() -> FakeEventBus:\n    return FakeEventBus()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef remote_gateway() -> FakeRemoteGateway:\n    return FakeRemoteGateway()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\nasync def services(event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway):\n    \"\"\"\n    Spin-up *two* sync services to simulate two independent micro-front-ends\n    running in different processes.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n    svc_b = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n\n    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.start(), svc_b.start())\n    yield svc_a, svc_b\n\n    await asyncio.gather(svc_a.stop(), svc_b.stop())\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_local_update_propagates_to_remote_and_peers(\n    services, event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    When one service updates the local profile, the change should:\n\n      1. Appear on the remote gateway.\n      2. Be broadcast on the local event bus.\n      3. Update *other* micro-front-ends listening for that user.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, svc_b = services\n    bus_q = event_bus.listen(\"profile.alice.updated\")\n\n    # Act – change a preference on service A.\n    svc_a.update_local({\"theme\": \"dark\"})\n\n    # 1) Wait for the bus broadcast and verify the payload.\n    msg = await wait_for(bus_q, predicate=lambda m: m.get(\"theme\") == \"dark\")\n    assert msg[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n    # 2) Eventually, remote profile should be in sync.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Allow async push to complete.\n    assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n    # 3) The peer (service B) must have updated its local copy.\n    assert svc_b.local_profile[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_last_write_wins_conflict_resolution(\n    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    When concurrent updates occur, the service implementing a *later* change\n    (higher `_last_modified`) must win the conflict.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, svc_b = services\n\n    # Simulate near-simultaneous changes.\n    svc_a.update_local({\"language\": \"en\"})\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n    svc_b.update_local({\"language\": \"fr\"})  # <- later write wins.\n\n    # Give the sync loop a moment to converge.\n    await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n\n    snap = remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")\n    assert snap[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n    assert svc_a.local_profile[\"language\"] == svc_b.local_profile[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_offline_changes_are_synced_when_network_recovers(\n    services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Offline edits must be queued locally and flushed to the server once\n    connectivity is restored.\n    \"\"\"\n    svc_a, _ = services\n\n    # 1→```python
     2→"""
     3→flockdesk.modules.presence.main
     4→================================
     5→
     6→Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module
     7→subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains
     8→a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI
     9→implemented with an MVVM flavour.
    10→
    11→The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the
    12→FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.
    13→
    14→Copyright:
    15→    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors – MIT License
    16→"""
    17→from __future__ import annotations
    18→
    19→import asyncio
    20→import importlib
    21→import logging
    22→import pkg_resources
    23→import signal
    24→import sys
    25→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    26→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    27→from pathlib import Path
    28→from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol
    29→
    30→from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot
    31→from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap
    32→from PySide6.QtWidgets import (
    33→    QApplication,
    34→    QLabel,
    35→    QListWidget,
    36→    QListWidgetItem,
    37→    QVBoxLayout,
    38→    QWidget,
    39→)
    40→
    41→LOG = logging.getLogger("flockdesk.presence")
    42→LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    43→
    44→
    45→################################################################################
    46→# Event-Bus Abstractions
    47→################################################################################
    48→class EventBus(Protocol):
    49→    """Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about."""
    50→
    51→    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...
    52→
    53→    def subscribe(
    54→        self,
    55→        topic: str,
    56→        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
    57→        *,
    58→        buffered: bool = False,
    59→    ) -> None: ...
    60→
    61→
    62→class _InProcessBus:
    63→    """
    64→    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and
    65→    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will
    66→    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).
    67→    """
    68→
    69→    def __init__(self) -> None:
    70→        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}
    71→
    72→    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    73→        LOG.debug("Publish: %s -> %s", topic, payload)
    74→        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):
    75→            try:
    76→                await handler(payload)
    77→            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
    78→                LOG.exception("Handler error for topic %s", topic)
    79→
    80→    def subscribe(
    81→        self,
    82→        topic: str,
    83→        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
    84→        *,
    85→        buffered: bool = False,
    86→    ) -> None:
    87→        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)
    88→        LOG.debug("Subscribed %s to %s", handler, topic)
    89→
    90→
    91→################################################################################
    92→# Model
    93→################################################################################
    94→@dataclass(slots=True)
    95→class UserPresence:
    96→    """Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence."""
    97→
    98→    user_id: str
    99→    display_name: str
   100→    status: str  # e.g. "online", "away", "dnd", "offline"
   101→    last_active: datetime
   102→    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None
   103→
   104→    def is_online(self) -> bool:
   105→        """Return ``True`` if the user is considered online."""
   106→        if self.status in {"offline", "unknown"}:
   107→            return False
   108→        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active
   109→        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)
   110→
   111→
   112→################################################################################
   113→# View-Model
   114→################################################################################
   115→class PresenceViewModel(QObject):
   116→    """
   117→    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.
   118→    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.
   119→    """
   120→
   121→    presence_changed = Signal()
   122→
   123→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   124→        super().__init__()
   125→        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}
   126→
   127→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API
   128→    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:
   129→        return sorted(
   130→            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)
   131→        )
   132→
   133→    @Slot(dict)
   134→    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   135→        """
   136→        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and
   137→        emits a notification to the UI controls.
   138→        """
   139→        user = UserPresence(
   140→            user_id=evt["user_id"],
   141→            display_name=evt.get("display_name", evt["user_id"]),
   142→            status=evt.get("status", "unknown"),
   143→            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt["last_active"]),
   144→            avatar_url=evt.get("avatar_url"),
   145→        )
   146→        LOG.debug("Presence updated: %s", user)
   147→        self._users[user.user_id] = user
   148→        self.presence_changed.emit()
   149→
   150→    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics
   151→    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover
   152→        return f"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>"
   153→
   154→
   155→################################################################################
   156→# Qt View
   157→################################################################################
   158→class PresenceListWidget(QWidget):
   159→    """
   160→    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.
   161→    It is intentionally minimal – complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's
   162→    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.
   163→    """
   164→
   165→    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):
   166→        super().__init__(parent)
   167→        self._vm = vm
   168→        self._setup_ui()
   169→        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)
   170→
   171→    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI
   172→    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:
   173→        self.setWindowTitle("Presence")
   174→        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
   175→        self._list = QListWidget(self)
   176→        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)
   177→        layout.addWidget(self._list)
   178→
   179→    @Slot()
   180→    def _refresh(self) -> None:
   181→        LOG.debug("Refresh UI")
   182→        self._list.clear()
   183→        for user in self._vm.all_users():
   184→            item = QListWidgetItem()
   185→            prefix = "🟢" if user.is_online() else "⚫"
   186→            item.setText(f"{prefix}  {user.display_name}")
   187→            if user.avatar_url:
   188→                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)
   189→                if not pixmap.isNull():
   190→                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))
   191→            self._list.addItem(item)
   192→
   193→
   194→################################################################################
   195→# Presence Service (async)
   196→################################################################################
   197→class PresenceService:
   198→    """
   199→    Background asyncio component responsible for:
   200→
   201→        • Subscribing to the presence topic
   202→        • Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)
   203→    """
   204→
   205→    _TOPIC = "users.presence"
   206→
   207→    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:
   208→        self._bus = bus
   209→        self._vm = vm
   210→        self._qt_obj = qt_obj
   211→
   212→    async def start(self) -> None:
   213→        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users
   214→        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)
   215→
   216→        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)
   217→        await self._publish_local_presence("online")
   218→
   219→        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive
   220→        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())
   221→
   222→    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals
   223→    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   224→        """
   225→        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt
   226→        thread where the view-model lives.
   227→        """
   228→        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we
   229→        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.
   230→        LOG.debug("Event-bus -> Qt: %s", payload)
   231→        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
   232→            self._qt_obj,
   233→            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),
   234→            Qt.QueuedConnection,
   235→        )
   236→
   237→    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:
   238→        payload = {
   239→            "user_id": self._local_user(),
   240→            "display_name": self._local_user(),  # demo
   241→            "status": status,
   242→            "last_active": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
   243→            "avatar_url": None,
   244→        }
   245→        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)
   246→
   247→    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:
   248→        while True:
   249→            await asyncio.sleep(30)
   250→            await self._publish_local_presence("online")
   251→
   252→    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util
   253→    @staticmethod
   254→    def _local_user() -> str:
   255→        # In real life we would ask the identity service
   256→        return Path.home().name
   257→
   258→
   259→################################################################################
   260→# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)
   261→################################################################################
   262→class PresenceProvider(Protocol):
   263→    """
   264→    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into
   265→    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python
   266→    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``
   267→    entry-point.
   268→    """
   269→

"""
flockdesk.modules.presence.main
================================

Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module
subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains
a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI
implemented with an MVVM flavour.

The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the
FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.

NOTE: This module now supports the unified UserService as the primary source
of presence data. The old event-bus-only approach is retained for backwards
compatibility when UserService is not available.

Copyright:
    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors – MIT License
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import importlib
import logging
import pkg_resources
import signal
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol, TYPE_CHECKING

from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot
from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap
from PySide6.QtWidgets import (
    QApplication,
    QLabel,
    QListWidget,
    QListWidgetItem,
    QVBoxLayout,
    QWidget,
)

# Import the unified UserService when available
if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService as _UserService

try:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService
    _HAS_USER_SERVICE = True
except ImportError:
    UserService = None  # type: ignore
    _HAS_USER_SERVICE = False

"""
flockdesk.modules.presence.main
================================

Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module
subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains
a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI
implemented with an MVVM flavour.

The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the
FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.

Copyright:
    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors – MIT License
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import importlib
import logging
import pkg_resources
import signal
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol

from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot
from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap
from PySide6.QtWidgets import (
    QApplication,
    QLabel,
    QListWidget,
    QListWidgetItem,
    QVBoxLayout,
    QWidget,
)

LOG = logging.getLogger("flockdesk.presence")
LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)


################################################################################
# Event-Bus Abstractions
################################################################################
class EventBus(Protocol):
    """Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about."""

    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...

    def subscribe(
        self,
        topic: str,
        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
        *,
        buffered: bool = False,
    ) -> None: ...


class _InProcessBus:
    """
    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and
    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will
    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}

    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        LOG.debug("Publish: %s -> %s", topic, payload)
        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):
            try:
                await handler(payload)
            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
                LOG.exception("Handler error for topic %s", topic)

    def subscribe(
        self,
        topic: str,
        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
        *,
        buffered: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)
        LOG.debug("Subscribed %s to %s", handler, topic)


################################################################################
# Model
################################################################################
@dataclass(slots=True)
class UserPresence:
    """Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence."""

    user_id: str
    display_name: str
    status: str  # e.g. "online", "away", "dnd", "offline"
    last_active: datetime
    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None

    def is_online(self) -> bool:
        """Return ``True`` if the user is considered online."""
        if self.status in {"offline", "unknown"}:
            return False
        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active
        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)


################################################################################
# View-Model
################################################################################
class PresenceViewModel(QObject):
    """
    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.
    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.
    """

    presence_changed = Signal()

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API
    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:
        return sorted(
            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)
        )

    @Slot(dict)
    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and
        emits a notification to the UI controls.
        """
        user = UserPresence(
            user_id=evt["user_id"],
            display_name=evt.get("display_name", evt["user_id"]),
            status=evt.get("status", "unknown"),
            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt["last_active"]),
            avatar_url=evt.get("avatar_url"),
        )
        LOG.debug("Presence updated: %s", user)
        self._users[user.user_id] = user
        self.presence_changed.emit()

    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics
    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover
        return f"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>"


################################################################################
# Qt View
################################################################################
class PresenceListWidget(QWidget):
    """
    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.
    It is intentionally minimal – complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's
    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.
    """

    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._vm = vm
        self._setup_ui()
        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI
    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:
        self.setWindowTitle("Presence")
        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
        self._list = QListWidget(self)
        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)
        layout.addWidget(self._list)

    @Slot()
    def _refresh(self) -> None:
        LOG.debug("Refresh UI")
        self._list.clear()
        for user in self._vm.all_users():
            item = QListWidgetItem()
            prefix = "🟢" if user.is_online() else "⚫"
            item.setText(f"{prefix}  {user.display_name}")
            if user.avatar_url:
                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)
                if not pixmap.isNull():
                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))
            self._list.addItem(item)


################################################################################
# Presence Service (async)
################################################################################
class PresenceService:
    """
    Background asyncio component responsible for:

        • Subscribing to the presence topic
        • Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)
    """

    _TOPIC = "users.presence"

    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:
        self._bus = bus
        self._vm = vm
        self._qt_obj = qt_obj

    async def start(self) -> None:
        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users
        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)

        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)
        await self._publish_local_presence("online")

        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive
        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals
    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt
        thread where the view-model lives.
        """
        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we
        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.
        LOG.debug("Event-bus -> Qt: %s", payload)
        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
            self._qt_obj,
            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),
            Qt.QueuedConnection,
        )

    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:
        payload = {
            "user_id": self._local_user(),
            "display_name": self._local_user(),  # demo
            "status": status,
            "last_active": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "avatar_url": None,
        }
        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)

    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:
        while True:
            await asyncio.sleep(30)
            await self._publish_local_presence("online")

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util
    @staticmethod
    def _local_user() -> str:
        # In real life we would ask the identity service
        return Path.home().name


################################################################################
# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)
################################################################################
class PresenceProvider(Protocol):
    """
    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into
    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python
    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``
    entry-point.
    """

    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...


def _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:
    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []
    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points("flockdesk_presence_provider"):
        try:
            factory = entry_point.load()
            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]
            LOG.info("Loaded presence provider %s", entry_point.name)
        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
            LOG.exception("Failed to load presence provider %s", entry_point.name)
    return providers


################################################################################
# Bootstrap
################################################################################
async def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:
    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)

    vm = PresenceViewModel()
    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)
    widget.resize(240, 400)
    widget.show()

    # Async service & plugins
    service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)
    await service.start()

    for prov in _load_providers():
        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop
    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so
    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    qtimer = QTimer()
    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms
    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake
    qtimer.start()

    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix
    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):
        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)

    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)


def main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover
    """
    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is
    executed directly.
    """
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.INFO,
        format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s",
    )
    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback
    try:
        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        LOG.info("Shutting down.")


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    main()

################################################################################
# Presence Service (async)
################################################################################
class PresenceService:
    """
    Background asyncio component responsible for:

        • Subscribing to the presence topic
        • Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)

    NOTE: When UserService is available, this service delegates to it for
    presence management. The event-bus fallback is retained for backwards
    compatibility.
    """

    _TOPIC = "users.presence"

    def __init__(
        self,
        bus: EventBus,
        vm: PresenceViewModel,
        qt_obj: QObject,
        user_service: Optional["_UserService"] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._bus = bus
        self._vm = vm
        self._qt_obj = qt_obj
        self._user_service = user_service

    async def start(self) -> None:
        # If UserService is available, use it as the primary source
        if self._user_service is not None:
            LOG.info("Using unified UserService for presence management")
            self._user_service.register_presence_listener(self._on_user_service_presence)
            self._user_service.start()
        else:
            # Fall back to event-bus subscription
            LOG.info("UserService not available, using event-bus fallback")
            self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)

        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)
        await self._publish_local_presence("online")

        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive
        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())

    def _on_user_service_presence(self, presence_info: Any) -> None:
        """Handle presence updates from the unified UserService."""
        payload = {
            "user_id": presence_info.user_id,
            "display_name": presence_info.user_id,
            "status": str(presence_info.state),
            "last_active": presence_info.updated_at.isoformat(),
            "avatar_url": None,
        }
        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
            self._qt_obj,
            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),
            Qt.QueuedConnection,
        )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals
    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt
        thread where the view-model lives.
        """
        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we
        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.
        LOG.debug("Event-bus -> Qt: %s", payload)
        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
            self._qt_obj,
            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),
            Qt.QueuedConnection,
        )

    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:
        # If UserService is available, use it to set status
        if self._user_service is not None:
            self._user_service.set_status(status)
            return

        # Fall back to event-bus
        payload = {
            "user_id": self._local_user(),
            "display_name": self._local_user(),  # demo
            "status": status,
            "last_active": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "avatar_url": None,
        }
        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)

    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:
        while True:
            await asyncio.sleep(30)
            await self._publish_local_presence("online")

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util
    @staticmethod
    def _local_user() -> str:
        # In real life we would ask the identity service
        return Path.home().name

"""
flockdesk.modules.presence.main
================================

Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module
subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains
a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI
implemented with an MVVM flavour.

The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the
FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.

NOTE: This module now supports the unified UserService as the primary source
of presence data. The old event-bus-only approach is retained for backwards
compatibility when UserService is not available.

Copyright:
    (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors – MIT License
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import importlib
import logging
import pkg_resources
import signal
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol, TYPE_CHECKING

from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot
from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap
from PySide6.QtWidgets import (
    QApplication,
    QLabel,
    QListWidget,
    QListWidgetItem,
    QVBoxLayout,
    QWidget,
)

# Import the unified UserService when available
if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService as _UserService

try:
    from flockdesk.core.services.user_service import UserService
    _HAS_USER_SERVICE = True
except ImportError:
    UserService = None  # type: ignore
    _HAS_USER_SERVICE = False

LOG = logging.getLogger("flockdesk.presence")
LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)


################################################################################
# Event-Bus Abstractions
################################################################################
class EventBus(Protocol):
    """Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about."""

    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...

    def subscribe(
        self,
        topic: str,
        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
        *,
        buffered: bool = False,
    ) -> None: ...


class _InProcessBus:
    """
    Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and
    single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will
    be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}

    async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        LOG.debug("Publish: %s -> %s", topic, payload)
        for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):
            try:
                await handler(payload)
            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
                LOG.exception("Handler error for topic %s", topic)

    def subscribe(
        self,
        topic: str,
        handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],
        *,
        buffered: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)
        LOG.debug("Subscribed %s to %s", handler, topic)


################################################################################
# Model
################################################################################
@dataclass(slots=True)
class UserPresence:
    """Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence."""

    user_id: str
    display_name: str
    status: str  # e.g. "online", "away", "dnd", "offline"
    last_active: datetime
    avatar_url: Optional[str] = None

    def is_online(self) -> bool:
        """Return ``True`` if the user is considered online."""
        if self.status in {"offline", "unknown"}:
            return False
        delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active
        return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)


################################################################################
# View-Model
################################################################################
class PresenceViewModel(QObject):
    """
    Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.
    The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.
    """

    presence_changed = Signal()

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API
    def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:
        return sorted(
            self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)
        )

    @Slot(dict)
    def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and
        emits a notification to the UI controls.
        """
        user = UserPresence(
            user_id=evt["user_id"],
            display_name=evt.get("display_name", evt["user_id"]),
            status=evt.get("status", "unknown"),
            last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt["last_active"]),
            avatar_url=evt.get("avatar_url"),
        )
        LOG.debug("Presence updated: %s", user)
        self._users[user.user_id] = user
        self.presence_changed.emit()

    # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics
    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover
        return f"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>"


################################################################################
# Qt View
################################################################################
class PresenceListWidget(QWidget):
    """
    Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.
    It is intentionally minimal – complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's
    global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.
    """

    def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._vm = vm
        self._setup_ui()
        vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI
    def _setup_ui(self) -> None:
        self.setWindowTitle("Presence")
        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)
        self._list = QListWidget(self)
        self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)
        layout.addWidget(self._list)

    @Slot()
    def _refresh(self) -> None:
        LOG.debug("Refresh UI")
        self._list.clear()
        for user in self._vm.all_users():
            item = QListWidgetItem()
            prefix = "🟢" if user.is_online() else "⚫"
            item.setText(f"{prefix}  {user.display_name}")
            if user.avatar_url:
                pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)
                if not pixmap.isNull():
                    item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))
            self._list.addItem(item)


################################################################################
# Presence Service (async)
################################################################################
class PresenceService:
    """
    Background asyncio component responsible for:

        • Subscribing to the presence topic
        • Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)
    """

    _TOPIC = "users.presence"

    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:
        self._bus = bus
        self._vm = vm
        self._qt_obj = qt_obj

    async def start(self) -> None:
        # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users
        self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)

        # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)
        await self._publish_local_presence("online")

        # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive
        asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals
    async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt
        thread where the view-model lives.
        """
        # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we
        # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.
        LOG.debug("Event-bus -> Qt: %s", payload)
        QMetaObject.invokeMethod(
            self._qt_obj,
            lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),
            Qt.QueuedConnection,
        )

    async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:
        payload = {
            "user_id": self._local_user(),
            "display_name": self._local_user(),  # demo
            "status": status,
            "last_active": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "avatar_url": None,
        }
        await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)

    async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:
        while True:
            await asyncio.sleep(30)
            await self._publish_local_presence("online")

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util
    @staticmethod
    def _local_user() -> str:
        # In real life we would ask the identity service
        return Path.home().name


################################################################################
# Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)
################################################################################
class PresenceProvider(Protocol):
    """
    Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into
    the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python
    packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``
    entry-point.
    """

    async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...


def _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:
    providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []
    for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points("flockdesk_presence_provider"):
        try:
            factory = entry_point.load()
            providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]
            LOG.info("Loaded presence provider %s", entry_point.name)
        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
            LOG.exception("Failed to load presence provider %s", entry_point.name)
    return providers


################################################################################
# Bootstrap
################################################################################
async def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:
    app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)

    vm = PresenceViewModel()
    widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)
    widget.resize(240, 400)
    widget.show()

    # Async service & plugins
    service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)
    await service.start()

    for prov in _load_providers():
        asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop
    # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so
    # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    qtimer = QTimer()
    qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms
    qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake
    qtimer.start()

    # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix
    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):
        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)

    await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)


def main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover
    """
    Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is
    executed directly.
    """
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.INFO,
        format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s",
    )
    bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback
    try:
        asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        LOG.info("Shutting down.")


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    main()
